mnemonics: Going Down Steps Carefully (GDSC): Gradient Descent Steps Carefully. Each step is proportional to the negative gradient, ensuring you move towards the minimum.
keyPoints: The core idea is to iteratively move in the direction of the steepest descent, which is the negative gradient.
The learning rate (step size) is a crucial hyperparameter; too high can cause overshooting, too low can cause slow convergence.
For convex functions, gradient descent is guaranteed to converge to the global minimum.
commonMistakes: Using a fixed, poorly chosen learning rate that causes the algorithm to diverge or converge very slowly.
Not ensuring the function is convex before applying the method, risking convergence to a local minimum instead of the global one.
